candidate_form: PENTA-RADIAL-AGG (5-way orthogonal partition with C5-equivariant aggregation imposing pentameric inductive bias)

results.title: Sand dollar — Wikipedia
results.notes: Source-domain reference only; no LLM mechanism.

results.title: Structured Hadamard Transforms for Attention
results.notes: Orthogonal Hadamard butterfly mixing, not cyclic-group equivariant, no 5-fold partition.

results.title: MAHA: Multiscale Aggregated Hierarchical Attention
results.notes: Depth-multiscale aggregation; no group-theoretic symmetry or radial partition.

results.title: Bayesian Geometry of Transformer Attention
results.notes: Orthogonal key bases, but Bayesian geometry framing; not C5-equivariant or pentameric.

results.title: Group-Equivariant Attention for Sequence Models
results.notes: Group-equivariant attention for cyclic permutations directly overlaps C5-equivariant aggregation mechanism; calibrated 0.75-0.85.

results.title: Cyclic Permutation Symmetry in Multi-Head Attention
results.notes: Cyclic permutation symmetry across heads; related but across-head rather than within-head petal partition; calibrated 0.65-0.75.

results.title: Equivariant Mixing Layer for Symmetric Attention
results.notes: Equivariant mixing for symmetry groups in attention; covers the C5-equivariant aggregation idea generically; calibrated 0.7-0.8.

results.title: DiHedral Equivariant Transformer
results.notes: Dihedral group equivariance for sequence models; dihedral D_n contains cyclic C_n, partially overlapping C5-equivariance; calibrated 0.65-0.75.

novelty_assessment: Four results (ranks 5-8) cover group-equivariant / cyclic-permutation / equivariant-mixing / dihedral-equivariant attention. These render the generic 'C5-equivariant attention aggregation' idea already covered. The specifically pentameric (5-fold, fixed-order) within-head petal-subspace decomposition with radial recomposition is not directly published, but the underlying mechanism (cyclic-group equivariant attention) is well-covered. Candidate is at best a narrow specialization (n=5) of existing group-equivariant attention work.
verdict: NOT NOVEL — generic C5/cyclic-group equivariant attention already covered by ranks 5-8.
